Audiences.Description: Intended consumer of a skill's behavior. customer = end users of the suite; general = applicable beyond marked Effortless projects.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical PK — kebab-cased audience label.

schema.name: AudienceLabel
schema.Description: Human label as written in SKILL.md frontmatter (audience: <label>).

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What this audience designation means.

schema.name: Skills
schema.Description: Skills tagged with this audience.

schema.name: CountOfSkills
schema.Description: How many skills declare this audience.

SkillCategories.Description: Functional grouping of skills as presented in the README. Categories are presentation/discovery groupings, not load-gate semantics (that's ScopeGateTypes).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical PK — kebab-cased category label.

schema.name: CategoryLabel
schema.Description: Display label as used in the README section header.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: What unifies the skills in this category.

schema.name: SortOrder
schema.Description: Presentation order in the README.

schema.name: Skills
schema.Description: Skills in this category.

schema.name: CountOfSkills
schema.Description: How many skills are in this category.

ScopeGateTypes.Description: When the harness loads a skill into context. The four patterns referenced in the skill-writing conventions (project-only / entry-point / tooling / theory).

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical PK — kebab-cased gate label.

schema.name: GateLabel
schema.Description: Short label for this load-gate pattern.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: When this gate fires — paraphrased from the skill description's Scope (load gate) clause.

schema.name: RequiresMarker
schema.Description: True iff the gate requires the project to be a marked Effortless project (effortless.json + ERB CLAUDE.md).

schema.name: LoadsOnExplicitRequest
schema.Description: True iff the gate also fires when the user explicitly invokes the skill by phrase, regardless of project marker.

schema.name: Skills
schema.Description: Skills using this gate pattern.

schema.name: CountOfSkills
schema.Description: How many skills use this gate pattern.

Skills.Description: One row per SKILL.md under skills/. The 23 currently live skills in the suite. Deprecation history is in DEPRECATED_SKILLS.md, not here.

schema.name: Name
schema.Description: Logical PK — equals the kebab-cased Slug, which equals the directory name.

schema.name: Slug
schema.Description: Skill folder name under skills/ — also the value of the name: field in SKILL.md frontmatter.

schema.name: Description
schema.Description: Short pitch — one sentence on what the skill is for.

schema.name: WhyItExists
schema.Description: The 'why' answer — what failure mode this skill prevents, paraphrased from the README.

schema.name: Category
schema.Description: Functional grouping (Orchestration, Project Lifecycle, etc.).

schema.name: ScopeGate
schema.Description: Load-gate pattern (when the harness pulls this skill into context).

schema.name: Audience
schema.Description: audience: value from SKILL.md frontmatter.

schema.name: DirectoryPath
schema.Description: Filesystem path to the skill directory, relative to repo root.

schema.name: CategoryLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent category.

schema.name: ScopeGateLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent scope-gate type.

schema.name: AudienceLabel
schema.Description: Display label of the parent audience.
